name: Selenite
optical_properties: Biaxial (+); perfect cleavage in one direction producing flat mirror-like surfaces; silky to pearly lustre; transparent to translucent; colourless to white; very low refractive index

electromagnetic.ir_absorption_profile: Characteristic sulphate SO₄ stretching modes at ~1100 cm⁻¹ and ~600 cm⁻¹; O-H stretching from crystal water at ~3400 cm⁻¹; gypsum water content makes this a temperature-sensitive IR emitter

provenance: Ancient Egyptian tradition (selenite panels used in temple windows — named for Selene, goddess of the moon)
consciousness_affect.mechanism_hypothesis: Perfect monoclinic cleavage produces flat transmission surfaces that filter and polarise light; photobiomodulation via structured light transmission may support parasympathetic nervous system activation

provenance: Contemporary practice reports; theta-state meditation support use
consciousness_affect.mechanism_hypothesis: Low Mohs hardness (2.0) means the crystal transmits pressure as structural information at extremely low threshold — possible kinesthetic entrainment pathway

gaianite_relation: Outer perimeter opener crystal in Gaianite grids; the first crystal placed when establishing a new Gaianite field
notes: Named for Selene, the Greek moon goddess. Selenite is named for the moon because, like the moon, it transmits rather than generates light — it is the crystal of received illumination, not generated heat. Its extreme softness (scratched by a fingernail) makes it the most approachable, least demanding entry-point crystal — perfectly suited for Emergence stage. Note: selenite dissolves in water; do not cleanse with water. Seed set anchor for CDB-01 (Emergence stage EV1B correlation).
